title: I shipped an MCP server for trading agents—free tier, hungry for first users + feedback
author: Jow-Dones
selftext:
Hey everyone — I’m the founder of **Jow Dones** (AI trader console at **jowdones.com**). I wanted something that actually fits how people use agents today: **one MCP surface** instead of duct-taping REST calls, spreadsheets, and browser tabs.

I’m opening the doors to **early users** (try without a credit card on the web app; tier copy and limits are on **/pricing**).

I’m especially looking for **feedback** from people wiring **Claude / Cursor / other MCP clients** into real workflows.

# What the MCP is for

* **Screen the market with a real query DSL** (fundamentals + technicals in one `search_instruments` query — think SP500 + RSI/MACD/MA conditions without N+1 indicator calls).
* **Regime + context**: market status, macro/earnings calendars, volatility/trend/correlation/dispersion reads, sector relative strength.
* **Deep data**: OHLCV + fundamentals history, batch indicators, single-symbol technicals.
* **Cross-asset helpers**: options chain / greeks / IV rank, futures contracts, FX rates — plus liquidity regime checks when you care about spreads.
* **Portfolio-aware workflows** (auth-gated): read/update portfolio notes, risk metrics, performance attribution, and a **manual order lifecycle** (save / approve / reject / mark executed / apply to portfolio) so agents can support *your* process — not autonomous trading.

# Full tool list (as of 2026-03-21)

**Market & events**  
`get_market_status`, `get_market_regime`, `get_economic_calendar`, `get_earnings_calendar`, `get_sector_ranking`

**Regime / structure**  
`get_volatility_regime`, `get_trend_regime`, `get_correlation_regime`, `get_dispersion`

**Universe & screens**  
`search_instruments`, `get_instrument`, `get_filter_options`

**Prices & fundamentals**  
`get_ohlcv_history`, `get_fundamentals_history`, `get_technical_indicators`, `get_batch_indicators`, `get_relative_strength`, `get_batch_relative_strength`

**Derivatives & FX**  
`get_option_chain`, `get_options_greeks`, `get_iv_rank`, `get_futures_contracts`, `get_fx_rate`

**Execution quality**  
`get_liquidity_regime`

**Portfolio & performance**  
`read_portfolio`, `update_portfolio`, `get_portfolio_risk_metrics`, `get_performance_attribution`

**Orders (manual / human-in-the-loop)**  
`save_order`, `approve_order`, `reject_order`, `get_orders`, `get_order`, `mark_order_executed`, `mark_order_not_executed`, `apply_order_to_portfolio`

# What I’d love from you

* **What workflow should I document first?** (e.g. “daily screen + earnings blackout + RS filter”, “options vol scan”, “portfolio risk before open”.)
* **What’s missing** in MCP that would make you delete a script permanently?
* **Pain points** connecting Cursor/Claude Desktop to a live account (auth, limits, ergonomics).

If you try it: tell me what client you used, what broke, and what felt magical. I read every serious note.

**Disclosure:** I built this. [**https://jowdones.com**](https://jowdones.com) — limits and tiers on **/pricing**. This post is about tooling and research workflows only; **not financial advice**; nothing here is a recommendation to buy or sell any security.

comments.author: stereotomyalan
comments.body: I will look, and thank you for the tool and free tier. Looks legit!

comments.author: Low_Blueberry_6711
comments.body: Nice work shipping this—MCP consolidation is a real pain point. One thing worth thinking about early: as your users wire this into real trading workflows, you'll want visibility into what the agent is actually executing (especially given financial risk). Have you thought about runtime monitoring for things like unexpected trade sizes, market conditions changing mid-execution, or cost overruns? Might be worth stress-testing some edge cases before users hit them in prod.
